arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --no-color
arguments.description: Disable ANSI color in output.

arguments.name: --quiet
arguments.description: Suppress non-error output.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation prompts (use with care).

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --no-banner
arguments.description: Suppress banner / tool-name output.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with vikramaadityam/SMPL_CS_PRJ_05_Exploiting_Android_with_Metasploit_Framwork (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with nowsecure/Exploiting-Android-WebViews-with-Frida (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with matheusado/Exploiting-Android-with-Metasploit (score=9); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['shell_android', 'shell_ios', 'web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['shell_android', 'shell_ios', 'web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['shell_android', 'shell_ios', 'web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_android, shell_ios, web] in the Mobile Security category. Fits when the engagement needs: 1 ) Download andriod https://developer.android.com/studio.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: 1 ) Download andriod https://developer.android.com/studio.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `frida -Uf learn.frida.unity --realm=emulated --no-pause`. 1 ) Download andriod https://developer.android.com/studio.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Mobile Security, use Frida-with-Unity-app-emulator because 1 ) Download andriod https://developer.android.com/studio.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `frida -Uf learn.frida.unity --realm=emulated --no-pause` as documented upstream for Frida-with-Unity-app-emulator.
When chaining Mobile Security tools, pair Frida-with-Unity-app-emulator with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `Frida-with-Unity-app-emulator --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
